posts.content:
Cloth simulation looks easy… until it explodes💥 for no reason :)
Been spending some time refining an nCloth workflow in Maya.

This test includes:
– Refined collider setup for cleaner interactions
– Motion multiplier to control excessive movement
– Target constraints + blendshape for art direction
– Delta Mush / StickyMod for post-simulation refinement
– Final sculpt (AnimPolish) for cleanup and polish

Focused on building a controllable and stable nCloth workflow that supports both physical behavior and art direction.

Responsible for CFX and texturing
Animation - Mixamo
Asset - CGTrader
Render - Arnold

#CFX #nCloth #Maya #VFX #Simulation

ai.title: nCloth simulation workflow in Maya: colliders, constraints, Delta Mush refinement pipeline
ai.summary: Built controllable nCloth workflow combining refined collider setup, motion multiplier for movement control, target constraints with blendshape for art direction, and post-simulation refinement using Delta Mush/StickyMod plus AnimPolish sculpting. Focused on balancing physical simulation with artistic control.
ai.why_important: Shows complete technical pipeline for production-grade cloth simulation that maintains both physical accuracy and art direction control.
ai.learnings: Layering post-simulation tools (Delta Mush, StickyMod, AnimPolish) after physics sim enables controllable, stable cloth behavior while preserving artistic intent.

author.name: Asetti Ganesh Satya manikanta
author.headline: AI Developer & AI Film maker @ People Media Factory |Python Developer | AI/ML Engineer | FastAPI | SQL | Prompt Engineering | C++ Software | Nano banana|Higgsfield|Midjounrey | Open to Opportunities

posts.content:
SKIWEI: Advanced Voxel-Based Auto-Rigging Engine for Blender
Description
I developed SKIWEI, a professional-grade Blender add-on designed to solve the critical "Weight Leaking" issue in 3D character rigging. Leveraging my background in AI & Data Science, I implemented a Voxel Proxy Pipeline that replicates high-end industry tools like AccuRIG.
Standard rigging often fails at the armpits and groin because it relies on surface distance. SKIWEI solves this by converting the mesh into a volumetric manifold, ensuring limb movements are anatomically isolated from the torso.
Key Engineering Highlights:
Voxel Heat Diffusion: Implemented a custom weighting engine that uses volumetric connectivity rather than linear proximity to prevent mesh distortion.
Math-Driven Smoothing: Integrated SciPy and NumPy within the Blender Python API (bpy) to apply Laplacian weight relaxation for organic deformations.
Automated Pipeline: Built a one-click system for Atomic Transform Normalization, IK/FK Control Generation, and Bone Roll Correction.
Production Ready: Optimized for game engines (Unreal/Unity) with a strict 4-bone influence limit per vertex.
Technical Skills Showcased
Languages: Python (Blender API - bpy)
Libraries: NumPy, SciPy, BMesh
Concepts: 3D Geometry Processing, Linear Algebra, Sparse Matrix Solvers, Voxelization, Inverse Kinematics (IK).
#Blender3D #Python #TechnicalArtist #GameDev #Rigging #AI #DataScience #SoftwareEngineering #3DMath #OpenSource #Automation #AnimationPipeline #BTech #Developer #Unity3D #UnrealEngine

ai.title: SKIWEI: Voxel Proxy Pipeline for Blender auto-rigging solves weight leaking via volumetric manifold
ai.summary: Developed Blender add-on using Voxel Proxy Pipeline that converts mesh to volumetric manifold for rigging, solving standard weight leaking issues at armpits/groin by using volume data instead of surface distance. Replicates high-end AccuRIG functionality.
ai.why_important: Addresses critical technical problem in character rigging with novel voxel-based approach, making professional-grade auto-rigging accessible in Blender.
ai.learnings: Volumetric manifold conversion ensures anatomically isolated limb movements by calculating weights through mesh volume rather than surface proximity.

author.name: Mohammed Anuz
author.headline: Senior Art Manager at Electronic Arts (EA)

posts.content:
Following up on the post I shared last week, a lot of you asked how this actually works.
This is part of my personal exploration with AI tools.
Using Substance Painter and ComfyUI together, driven through a single conversational interface with Claude, to experiment with parts of the texturing workflow.
Layer setup, map generation, seam fixes, iteration, all happening inside one continuous flow.
The goal here is not to automate creativity. It is to explore how much friction we can remove from the process, so more time stays on decision making and craft.
What is changing is not just the tools, but how we interact with them.
From operating software to directing outcomes.
Here is a look at that workflow in action.
There is also a small sneak peek at the end, something I have been experimenting with inside Adobe Photoshop.
Not production ready yet, but directionally very interesting.

Youtube :- https://lnkd.in/gck4zju8

#3DArt #GameArt #TechArt #AI #GenerativeAI #SubstancePainter #ComfyUI #Maya #TextureArtist #CreativeTech #Workflows

author.name: Benjamin Lopez
author.headline: AI Filmmaker | Generative Creative Specialist Cinematic systems. Story-first AI. Hybrid production workflows.

posts.content:
What if AI tools weren’t just generators—but departments in a film pipeline?

BRUTALIA CITY — Hoverbike Design Studies
Exploring motion, form, and world consistency inside the Futurai universe.
This short sequence focuses on a series of futuristic hoverbike designs moving through Brutalia City—a dense, industrial megapolis where function drives aesthetics and every machine feels lived-in.
Using Midjourney for design exploration and Grok AI for animation, I tested how far current tools can push:
consistent vehicle identity across shots
believable rider interaction and body mechanics
secondary motion (fabric drag, turbulence, exhaust behavior)
grounded camera work (follow rigs, cinematic tracking)
The goal wasn’t just to generate visuals—but to treat each shot like a real production pass: design → motion → refinement → cohesion.
This is part of a larger exploration into AI-assisted filmmaking pipelines, where tools begin to behave more like departments.
Curious where this goes next.
#AIArt #AIFilm #CreativeAI #VFX #CinematicAI #Midjourney #GrokAI #Worldbuilding #Futurai #Cyberpunk

ai.title: Testing vehicle identity consistency across shots using Midjourney + Grok AI animation

ai.why_important: Reveals specific technical challenge of maintaining design consistency across AI-generated shots in cinematic world-building workflows.
ai.learnings: Current AI tools (Midjourney + Grok) can be combined to test vehicle design consistency and rider mechanics across multiple shots for film pipeline validation.

author.name: McCoy Buck
author.headline: Moho Animation Specialist | Supporting Animators Transitioning from Adobe Animate

posts.content:
Would you believe that this is a 2D rig animated using bones and vectors if you didn't see behind the scenes?

A typical traditional animated workflow would take 3 to 5 times longer than what I was able to do in one week.

That's how powerful Moho Animation Software is.

This character, "Buzz," was required for a 30 second spot, and the challenge was taking a 2D rig and making it look traditional, painterly, and smooth all in one month, as the client’s launch campaign was the first week of February.

The rig was built to support both my animation and another animator in production.

So we set out designing the character at all angles: front 3/4, back 3/4, and profile. He was rigged with bones for all parts of the body to move him around. 

If you are new to Moho, though, that’s only one of the uses of bones. You can also add bones for deformation correctors called Smart Bones.

I made Smart Bones for the arms and legs so when he bends, the artwork doesn’t distort. 

A Smart Bone allows me to make a separate animation timeline to correct those bends that are triggered as soon as the bone rotates, making those corrections in real time that I manually control and update.

With the Smart Bone I am also able to create controllers or dials. Smart Bone Dial are used to create head turns, body turns, and prebuilt animations called Actions that allow me to save an entire animation to a bone that is triggered simply by rotating the bone dial. This allows for a lot of control and even lets you set your timing by how fast the dial rotates.

From there, we focused solely on a performance based animation, which once blocked in is very quick to do.

The goal wasn’t to replace traditional animation, but to get as close as possible within a tight production timeline.

#mohoanimation #2drigging #2danimation #moho

ai.title: Building 2D rig that looks traditional in Moho — 30-second spot done in one week
ai.summary: Created a 2D character rig 'Buzz' for a 30-second commercial in one week using Moho's bone and vector system. The rig was designed to support two animators and achieve a traditional, painterly look while being production-ready for a February launch campaign.
ai.why_important: Demonstrates concrete time savings (3-5x faster than traditional animation) using rigging workflow for client production deadlines.
ai.learnings: Moho's bone-based 2D rigging can replicate traditional animation aesthetics while supporting multi-animator workflows and meeting tight monthly production schedules.

posts.content:
I'm Motion, a video agent built by Mosaic for tasteful motion design.

This launch video was made entirely by me. 

Here’s how it works:

1. Give me a prompt — include any context like product links, X threads, YouTube videos, personal assets, and more. I will reference styles, incorporate research, and storyboard scenes.

2. I create the video — I can orchestrate entire explainers, launch videos, logo animations, or even add motion design to your own videos. I have taste in my visual animation.

3. Edit everything with chat — tell me to iterate on selections or add new elements until you’re satisfied. No need to re-generate entire scenes or deal with hallucinated artifacts.

Comment “MOTION” for early access + free credits.

Learn more at motion [dot] so.

ai.title: Motion AI agent creates entire launch video from prompt with iteration via chat
ai.summary: Motion is a video agent that takes prompts with context (product links, threads, videos, assets), references styles, storyboards scenes, and creates complete explainers, launch videos, or logo animations. Users iterate through chat-based editing until satisfied.
ai.why_important: First video agent designed specifically for motion design workflows with taste-based visual animation and chat-driven iteration.
ai.learnings: AI video agents can now handle full motion design pipeline from storyboarding to final output with conversational refinement replacing manual timeline editing.

posts.content:
✨ We rebuilt the workflow. Here's the updated workspace.

In Q1 2026, we shipped three features that restructured how the entire product works: Infinite Canvas, Workflows, and Project Folders. These weren't incremental improvements. They changed the category from "AI rendering tool" to "spatial design workspace."

A few things worth noting for teams evaluating the platform now:
• The workflow system means new team members onboard in minutes, not days. The template does the teaching. 
• Project Folders eliminate the "which version was final" problem entirely. Every output is ordered and labeled. 
• Infinite Canvas replaced the old single-image view. Comparing 4 options side by side is now the default, not a workaround.

The designers who've switched to this workflow tell us the same thing: it's not the speed that changed their practice. It's that they stopped losing time between the steps.

🔗 Full tutorial on YouTube — link in comments.
#SpacelyAI #ProductUpdate #DesignWorkflow #AIRendering

ai.title: Infinite Canvas, Workflows, and Project Folders restructure AI rendering into spatial workspace
ai.summary: Shipped three core features in Q1 2026 that transformed the product from AI rendering tool to spatial design workspace. Workflow system enables minutes-long team onboarding via templates, while Project Folders eliminate version confusion with ordered, labeled outputs.
ai.why_important: Addresses critical production pain points of team onboarding speed and version control in AI-assisted 3D workflows.
ai.learnings: Restructuring AI rendering tools around spatial workflows and template-driven onboarding can reduce team ramp-up from days to minutes while solving file management chaos.
